stories.title: Ukraine war briefing: Protests of Fedorov’s dismissal continue as Zelenskyy tries to repair rift with Poland
stories.summary: Thousands gather outside ⁠presidential office for second day, following surprise government reshuffle. What we know on day 1,606 For a second day, thousands of Ukrainians have taken to the streets to protest against the sudden removal of popular and innovative defence minister , Mykhailo Fedorov, who has clashed with the more conventional military chief of staff, Oleksandr Syrskyi. Several thousand people gathered outside ⁠the presidential office after Fedorov was not reappointed in the surprise

stories.title: Trump threatens Canada over wildfire smoke choking US cities
stories.summary: A haze fell over the Capitol in Washington on Friday as smoke from Canadian wildfires stretched farther east, prompting the president and Republican lawmakers to level blame at Canada for forestry practices they said were wrecking U.S. air quality. U.S. President Donald Trump and congressional Republicans harangued the Canadian government for failing to remove excess timber and brush that fuels blazes like the ones now burning in northern Ontario. The Carney government, they said, is responsible

stories.title: Hungarian president fires himself, bowing out under Magyar’s pressure
stories.summary: Hungarian President Tamás Sulyok signed a constitutional amendment on Saturday that will remove him from office. Sulyok had resisted months of public pressure from Prime Minister Péter Magyar, who called for the president’s resignation as part of his efforts to dismantle what he calls the “mafia” that his predecessor, Viktor Orbán, created while in office. But after a constitutional amendment designed to remove Sulyok from office passed on Monday, the Orbán-appointed president’s lega

stories.title: Cyclospora: iceberg lettuce recalled in 27 states and more products may follow
stories.summary: Taylor Farms does not specify where products were served or sold, as US braces for weeks more of outbreak Taylor Farms recalled potentially contaminated shredded iceberg lettuce in 27 states on Friday, including lettuce distributed as recently as Thursday, as cases of cyclosporiasis continue rising in the US. The US is likely to see at least another two weeks of possible cases, since infections may have happened in recent days. And the expanding outbreak investigation could point to other produc

stories.title: Hungary’s president agrees to stand down after law change ends his term
stories.summary: Tamás Sulyok signed the constitutional amendment that cited ‘serious loss of confidence’ in him as leader Hungary’s president, Tamás Sulyok, has agreed to step down after signing a constitutional amendment passed by the ruling Tisza party of the prime minister, Péter Magyar. The amendment will end Sulyok’s term immediately, citing society’s “serious loss of confidence” in a leader elected in early 2024 by lawmakers from the former prime minister Viktor Orbán’s nationalist Fidesz party. Continue

stories.title: Hungary's president signs amendment ending his own term
stories.summary: An ally of former PM Viktor Orban has signed a constitutional amendment ending his own term as head of state. Tamas Sulyok criticized the law, while lawmakers insist he has impeded national progress by staying in office.

stories.title: Hungarian president Tamás Sulyok signs constitutional amendment ending his own mandate
stories.summary: President Tamás Sulyok signed the seventeenth amendment to the Fundamental Law on Saturday, he announced on Facebook. The sitting president’s mandate will end the day after it takes effect.

stories.title: Hungary's president agrees to stand down after parliament backs removal
stories.summary: Tamás Sulyok, seen as an Orbán loyalist, had until Saturday night to agree to end his term.

stories.title: German conservative parliamentary leader resigns over surrogacy debate
stories.summary: BRUSSELS — Jens Spahn, a senior lawmaker with German Chancellor Friedrich Merz Christian Democrats, resigned from his post as the party’s parliamentary leader on Saturday amid controversy over his use of a surrogate to have a child. Spahn came under growing pressure to step down after he revealed earlier this week that he and his husband had become parents using a surrogate mother in the United States, despite the practice being banned in Germany. “Over the past few days, I&#821

stories.title: Béranya igénybevétele miatt kellett lemondania a német kormánypárt frakcióvezetőjének
stories.summary: Jens Spahnnak és férjének az USA-ban született meg a gyermeke, miközben Németországban a béranyaság tiltott.

stories.title: Vietnám éppen Kína nyomdokaiba lép, megvalósíthatják azt, amivel Magyarország is próbálkozna
stories.summary: Tavaly több mint 8 százalékkal nőtt Vietnám gazdasága, és a kormány szerint ez csak a kezdet. Már sok ázsiai ország próbálta ezt a receptet, de még rengeteg pénzre és reformra lesz szükség, hogy sikerüljön a nagy fejlődési ugrás.

stories.title: Korai még temetni a 64 éves magyar csokigyárat
stories.summary: Csökken a kereslet az üreges csokoládéfigurák iránt, nehéz helyzetbe került az erre specializálódott diósgyőri gyár, de még van esély befektető bevonására.

stories.title: Orbán Viktor: Az önkény immár nem fenyegetés, hanem valóság
stories.summary: A volt miniszterelnök is reagált arra, hogy Sulyok aláírja a 17. Alaptörvény-módosítást.

stories.title: Magyar Péter: Jobb lett volna hamarabb, méltóbb módon, a köztársasági elnök lemondásával eljutni idáig
stories.summary: A miniszterelnök azt mondta, hogy az elnökjelöltet nem szivarszobákban, hanem a pártokkal, a közélet szereplőivel, civilekkel és magánemberekkel közösen szeretnék megtalálni.

stories.title: Hungarian president signs amendment to Fundamental Law ending his mandate
stories.summary: House Speaker Ágnes Forsthoffer will temporarily take over presidential duties. Parliament has thirty days to elect a new head of state.

stories.title: Kihirdették a Magyar Közlönyben a 17. Alaptörvény-módosítást
stories.summary: Azt már eddig is lehetett tudni, hogy Sulyok Tamás aláírta a módosítást, ezt ő maga jelentette be szombaton kora este a Facebook-oldalán közzétett videóban.

stories.title: Magyar Péter elárulta, hogy a Tisza milyen köztársasági elnököt szeretne
stories.summary: Az Országgyűlésnek 30 napon belül új köztársasági elnököt kell választania.

stories.title: Elillant
stories.summary: Több hónapos huzavona után távozott a köztársasági elnöki székből Sulyok Tamás. Az egyik legnépszerűtlenebb elnök hallgatott, amikor beszélhetett volna, de megszólalt, amikor jobb lett volna, ha hallgat.

stories.title: Az NB I.-ben nem lesz hidratációs szünet, a szájeltakarást sem büntetik olyan szigorúan, mint a foci-vb-n
stories.summary: Amúgy július 1-től a magyar pályákon is változtak kicsit a labdarúgás szabályai.

stories.title: Verbális abúzusról és ellehetetlenítésről ír Sulyok az Alaptörvény-módosítást kihirdető határozatában
stories.summary: Látja és tapasztalja, hogy mindezek a törekvések jelen pillanatban a magyar társadalom egy jelentős részének helyesnek tűnnek.

stories.title: A gyilkos a sírba vitte a titkát, de 25 év után sem adják fel, hogy megtalálják a brit turista holttestét
stories.summary: Úgy ítélték el a gyilkosát, hogy a holttest soha nem lett meg, és még olyanok is voltak, akik a megmenekült barátnő bűnösségét sejttették. A rendőrség most megpróbál új fülesekhez jutni.

stories.title: Kirúgták a MÁV-tól Orbán kampánykörútjának egyik résztvevőjét, akire a kampányban figyelt fel a Telex
stories.summary: Cserháti Gyula a Telex korábbi cikkei szerint rendszeres résztvevője volt a kampányban Orbán Viktor fórumainak. A miniszterelnököt éltető és az ellentüntetőket távoltartó csoporthoz tartozott.

stories.title: Weather tracker: Thunderstorms strike across Europe amid record heatwave
stories.summary: Storms are typical during intense heat but this week’s have been extreme. Plus, deadly monsoon rains in Bangladesh Hailstones the size of golf balls have been seen in French villages as, on top of the exceptional European heatwave, thunderstorms have struck across parts of Europe. While thunderstorms are typical during and after a period of extreme heat, the storms across countries such as France, Germany and Poland have been particularly severe, bringing flooding, strong winds and heavy showers

stories.title: A marosvécsi Helikon-centenárium körül áll a bál: Takaró Mihály miatt kulturális konfliktus lett az ünneplésből
stories.summary: Huszonhat kortárs erdélyi író mondta le részvételét a marosvécsi Helikon-centenáriumon, miután nyilvánossá vált, hogy a rendezvényen Takaró Mihály is előadást tartott volna.

stories.title: Tarr Zoltán reagált arra, hogy Orbán János Dénes visszavásárolná a műveit
stories.summary: A miniszter szerint ez már nem az az ország, amelyik háttéralkukkal intézi az ügyeit.

stories.title: Szétroppantott gerincek, spenótlében ázó lapok – horrorsztorik és szentimentális történetek kölcsönadott könyvekről
stories.summary: Hiába a Netflix, a podcastok és az e-olvasók, a legtöbb lakásban létezik valahol legalább egy kis sarok – máshol akár egész falak –, ahol könyvek sorakoznak. Románcok, kortárs regények, young adult könyvek, interjúkötetek, önéletrajzok vagy művészeti albumok. Ami biztos: ahol könyv van, ott kölcsönkapott könyv is akad. De jó ez nekünk?

stories.title: Börtön is járhat a megalázó, erőszakos tartalmak streameléséért Lengyelországban
stories.summary: Az ilyen közvetítések célja a nézettség, a mikroadományok vagy a hirdetési bevételek növelése.

stories.title: Magyar Péter: Tik-tak, tik-tak
stories.summary: Vélhetően Sulyok Tamásnak üzent, akinek már csak pár órája maradt, hogy aláírja az Alaptörvény-módosítást, amivel saját magát mozdítja el a pozíciójából.

stories.title: Taco Bell lettuce tied to diarrhea-causing parasite outbreak
stories.summary: US health officials linked iceberg lettuce served at Taco Bell to an outbreak of the parasite cyclospora, which causes “explosive” diarrhea. Health officials warn that the contamination may go beyond the fast-food chain.

stories.title: Hungary's national fireworks costs shrink but many still call it wasteful
stories.summary: Tradition or money down the drain? A state-funded jolly for all, or an outdated form of animal cruelty? As 20 August nears, the yearly row over whether Hungary needs fireworks and how big they should be reignites.

stories.title: Conservatives around world at war against ‘leftwing globalists’, Bridget McKenzie tells CPAC London event
stories.summary: Nationals senator echoes comments by Pauline Hanson as Labor criticises One Nation leader’s divisive stance Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The National party’s Senate leader, Bridget McKenzie, has used an appearance at a conference of the populist right in London to declare that conservatives are “in a war” against mass immigration, “leftwing globalists” and “woke” institutions, and to urge rightwing movements a

stories.title: Trump’s threats to revoke TV licenses get serious
stories.summary: President Donald Trump’s threats against broadcast television licenses are suddenly less of a laugh line to the media world and more of a real danger. Trump’s  broadsides Thursday night against NBC and ABC  were just the latest occasion during the past nine years when he’s called for revoking the licenses of TV outlets that anger him, this time after the two networks declined to air his White House speech on election security. “Fraud like this should mean a revocation of their lic

stories.title: Trump’s speech offered a preview of his next moves on elections
stories.summary: President Donald Trump’s elections legislation remains stalled in Congress. But his Thursday night primetime address contained hints of where he might take his whole-of-government approach to asserting control over American elections. The  president pointed to a trove of newly declassified documents  as proof that American elections are at risk from foreign influence and that the infrastructure they rely on is vulnerable to hacking. He furthermore characterized his own intelligenc

stories.title: Trump’s rhetoric is more dangerous than voting machine flaws, experts say
stories.summary: Election officials in the U.S. are warning that President Donald Trump’s assertion that America’s voting machines aren’t safe is far more dangerous than the vulnerabilities he highlighted in his Thursday night address. Flaws in electronic voting machines have been well-documented for more than a decade by researchers, and states have poured money into bolstering security. Still, none have ever been exploited by malicious actors to successfully change the outcome of an election. Now officials are

stories.title: Dismissal of Ukraine’s defence minister highlights wider issues for Zelenskyy
stories.summary: Mykhailo Fedorov, celebrated by many for innovative, tech-driven approach, was sidelined for military old guard Volodomyr Zelenskyy’s abrupt dismissal of Ukraine’s youthful and innovative defence minister, Mykhailo Fedorov, at precisely the moment Kyiv appeared to be gaining advantages in several spheres of its war with Russia has exposed, not for the first time, a troubling flaw in the president’s leadership. The move, which has startled senior European officials and caused consternation, and d

stories.title: Two US troops killed and one missing in Jordan after Iranian attacks
stories.summary: Sirens sound in Bahrain and Kuwait accuses Tehran of targeting civilian sites and infrastructure as Iran strikes US allies Two US troops were killed and one remains missing in Jordan after Iran launched a wave of attacks against US allies in the Middle East. Iran’s attacks came as the renewal of US strikes on Iran entered a second week and fighting escalated over the strait of Hormuz. Continue reading...

stories.title: Pauline Hanson tells London CPAC crowd she is ‘sick of hearing about white privilege’ and ‘fears for England’
stories.summary: Reform UK leader Nigel Farage compares One Nation leader’s polling rise to Donald Trump’s victory in the US Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Pauline Hanson has told a political conference in London that Australia is following Britain down a path of cultural decline, blaming mass immigration, multiculturalism, Islam, net zero policies and “woke” ideology such as support for trans rights. The One Nation leader has b

stories.title: Ukraine drones target Russian warehouses and oil sites
stories.summary: Ukrainian drones have struck warehouses, an oil depot and other targets across Russia, killing nine people and injuring over 80 more. Kyiv said its strikes targeted suppliers for Moscow's ongoing invasion of Ukraine.

stories.title: 2 US service members killed in Iranian attack on Jordan
stories.summary: Two American service members were killed in an Iranian ballistic missile and drone attack on Friday in Jordan, U.S. Central Command announced on social media Saturday. Another service member is unaccounted for. The deaths mark the latest flare-up in tensions since U.S. President Donald Trump declared the ceasefire between the U.S. and Iran had ended . Sixteen American service members have lost their lives in the fighting since the war started in February. “Four American service members were medi

stories.title: India: Police move activist Sonam Wangchuk to hospital on court orders
stories.summary: New Delhi's police have asked people to vacate the Jantar Mantar site where Wangchuk, students and supporters of the Cockroach Janat Party have been camping in protest of the education minister.

stories.title: Memphis rallies for progressive Democrat after four fatal shootings by taskforce: ‘fight of our lives’
stories.summary: Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez, Ayanna Pressley and Summer Lee join rally to support Justin Pearson’s bid for Congress More than a thousand people rallied for Justin Pearson, a Democratic state representative running for Congress, in the wake of four fatal shootings by members of Donald Trump’s Memphis Safe Task Force over the last two months. Crowds packed New Direction Christian church in the city’s Hickory Hill neighborhood in support of Pearson, who is running in the now-fractured ninth congressio

stories.title: Republican lawmaker Ralph Norman to join race to succeed Lindsey Graham in South Carolina
stories.summary: Congressman to run for seat despite Donald Trump backing Graham’s sister, Darline, should she choose to run The Republican representative Ralph Norman, ⁠of South Carolina , has announced he will join the race to succeed the late senator Lindsey Graham, despite Donald Trump backing Graham’s sister, Darline, should she choose to run. Announcing the launch of his bid on Saturday, Norman wrote on X: “I’m running to represent the people of South Carolina in the US Senate because we need a fighter who

stories.title: Trump, not Iran, is the world’s greatest danger. He’s a one-man weapon of mass destruction | Simon Tisdall
stories.summary: As the bombing starts again, it’s clear the president has dragged the US into a limitless fiasco – and the world into an economic quagmire Feckless and clueless, Donald Trump is lost in Iran, unable to find a way out of the disastrous war he started. Once again, the US military is pummelling the country and, increasingly, its civilian infrastructure. As before, this unlawful bludgeoning strengthens the resistance of a hardline regime that cares little for its people’s suffering. How often have T

stories.title: ‘It becomes inevitable’: the toxic mix fuelling deadly political violence around world
stories.summary: After killing of a British former MP, experts say dehumanising rhetoric, declining institutional trust and disinformation fuelling a global problem On 9 July, the body of Ann Widdecombe, an uncompromising, staunchly conservative former UK government minister turned TV personality and spokesperson for the radical-right Reform UK party, was found at her home in south-west England. Two days later, a man was arrested in South Yorkshire. Believed to be previously unknown to the local police force and

stories.title: Two US troops killed and one missing after Iranian attack in Jordan
stories.summary: Iran and the US have traded strikes as hostilities re-escalated this week, with Tehran targeting US military facilities across the Gulf region.

stories.title: Russian online retail warehouses hit by deadly Ukrainian strikes
stories.summary: Drones targeted Wildberries facilities near Moscow and in Tambov. Ukraine's leader called them "major logistics facilities" supplying "sanctioned components".

stories.title: Air quality warnings remain in place across US as wildfire smoke continues to swathe country
stories.summary: Rain could alleviate conditions in mid-Atlantic and north-east, with World Cup final expected to go ahead on Sunday Warnings of dangerous conditions are expected to remain in place on Saturday across swathes of the US, amid uncertainty about where the heavy wildfire smoke swirling from the Canadian province of Ontario and the US state of Minnesota will head next. Some parts of the US mid-Atlantic and north-east regions will continue to endure poor air quality until Saturday afternoon, where ther

stories.title: New footage shows assassination attempt on Ukrainian businessman in Monaco – video
stories.summary: Ukraine's prosecutor general, Ruslan Kravchenko, said investigators have recovered key evidence in the case of the attempted assassination of the Ukrainian businessman Vadym Iermolaiev in Monaco. Specialists from Ukraine's security services restored a surveillance-camera recording that suspects had allegedly attempted to destroy. According to the prosecutor general, the surveillance camera had been installed near the crime scene to obtain confirmation that the alleged contract killing had been c

stories.title: Scores of carers overpaid more than £20,000 last year despite reforms
stories.summary: DWP brought in measures to tackle carer’s allowance scandal yet in 2025-26 there were 32,559 overpayments Scores of unpaid carers were hit with demands to repay sums of more than £20,000 and hundreds more put at risk of prosecution last year as a result of official failures in what appear to be continuing problems with carer’s allowance. New figures showed carers were asked to repay £33m in 2025-26 as a result of 32,559 overpayments, despite the introduction of measures over a year ago designed

stories.title: Doctors question evidence behind Pentagon plan for testosterone screening
stories.summary: Pete Hegseth announced that soldiers aged 30 and older in the US military will be screened for low testosterone The US defense secretary, ⁠Pete Hegseth, this week ordered an annual testosterone-deficiency screening for active-duty and reserve service members aged 30 and older, which he says will help to maintain military readiness. But many medical professionals warn it might do nothing of the sort and instead could increase service members’ risk of infertility or lead to other consequences if t

stories.title: Germany’s CDU parliamentary leader resigns after using surrogacy to become parent
stories.summary: Jens Spahn had criticised ‘rented wombs’ and his party is strongly opposed to surrogacy, which is banned in Germany A senior German politician and ally of the chancellor, Friedrich Merz, has resigned as parliamentary group leader of the Christian Democrat (CDU) party after he and his husband used a surrogate mother to become parents, a practice he has criticised in the past and his party is vehemently opposed to. Surrogacy is banned in Germany, a policy Jens Spahn refused to relax when he was he

stories.title: ‘It’s only going to get worse’: wildfires forcing firefighters to make impossible choices
stories.summary: As the climate crisis fuels more intense blazes, pushing them to new parts of the world, those tackling them are forced to ration resources and decide which to fight César Alcaraz had only just become a firefighter in the late 1990s when he found himself ambushed by a fast-moving blaze. Barely able to breathe and with no more water left in his truck, he and his colleagues fled an inferno ravaging Spain’s Montgó mountain region, wishing their bosses had sent more support. But nearly three decades

stories.title: North Dakota men who discover they were switched as newborns sue hospital
stories.summary: Kyle Bylin and Jeremy Morrison uncovered the truth after Bylin received an at-home DNA test as a Christmas gift A DNA discovery has led two families to accuse a North Dakota hospital of changing the course of their lives after learning two newborns were allegedly switched at birth nearly four decades ago. Kyle Bylin uncovered the truth after receiving an at-home DNA test during a Christmas gift exchange. The test connected him with his biological aunt through a genealogy platform, prompting her

stories.title: Flash flood warnings strike north-east US amid wildfire smoke from Canada
stories.summary: Poor air quality from wildfires continues to plague New York City as several neighborhoods hit by thunderstorms Saturday is delivering a double dose of dangerous weather across the north-eastern US, with smoky skies from Canadian wildfires giving way to severe thunderstorms that have already triggered a flash flood warning as torrential rain pounds parts of the region. After a brief break on Friday, smoke from the wildfires moved back into New York City and surrounding communities on Saturday, s

stories.title: Burnham to scrap Starmer’s digital ID scheme in ‘reset of priorities’
stories.summary: Incoming PM will reallocate unspecified resources from unpopular plan to helping with cost of living Andy Burnham is expected to scrap Keir Starmer’s plans for digital ID cards in a “reset of priorities” when he enters Downing Street on Monday. The new Labour leader plans to redirect the resources earmarked for the scheme towards tackling the cost of living, his team indicated on Saturday. Continue reading...

stories.title: ANU accused of ‘hysterical’ response to students using AI to cheat as unis scramble to ‘secure’ assessments
stories.summary: Australia risks ‘shipping’ intellectual capability overseas if educational rigour not restored, experts warn Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast An academic at the Australian National University has accused it of a “hysterical” response to students using AI to cheat, as tertiary institutions rush to shore up the credibility of assessments. But a colleague warned Australia is in danger of “shipping our national intell

stories.title: Andrew and Tristan Tate arrested in Miami on charges of rape and sex trafficking
stories.summary: UK to seek extradition over sexual exploitation, ‘extreme pornography’ and assault and indecent images of a child Andrew and Tristan Tate, brothers who are social media influencers, were arrested by federal authorities Saturday in Miami, the US Marshals Service said. The charges against the pair were not immediately announced. Brady McCarron, a spokesperson for the marshals, said the warrant was sealed. Continue reading...

stories.title: Israeli ministers announce plans for new illegal settlements in Gaza and West Bank
stories.summary: Defence minister seeks three ‘Nahal’ outposts in Gaza as top commander says Israel now controls 65% of the strip, violating Trump ceasefire deal Israel’s defence and finance ministers announced plans for three illegal settlements in Gaza and more than $400m (£300m) in funding to expand construction in the occupied West Bank, as Israel’s military commander for the region celebrated violent outposts as his “security partners”. With national elections scheduled for 27 October, Benjamin Netanyahu’s

stories.title: US launches seventh night of Iran strikes as Hormuz conflict escalates
stories.summary: Central Command says attacks were designed to ‘continue degrading Iranian military capabilities’ The US military said it had launched a seventh consecutive night of strikes on Iran on Friday night as fighting escalated over the strait of Hormuz. US Central Command, in a post on X, said the strikes, which began at 7pm GMT, were designed to “continue degrading Iranian military capabilities”. Continue reading...

stories.title: Why is Trump risking midterm disaster by resuming the unpopular Iran war?
stories.summary: Less than a month after hailing a ceasefire, Trump resumed strikes on Iran, a move experts warn could prolong the war and hurt Republicans before the midterms For half a century, Donald Trump has performed a public high-wire act based on high-stakes risks and shattering time-honored norms to get what he wants. The approach has paid off handsomely, helping him survive multiple bankruptcies to reach billionaire status and numerous legal and political scandals to be elected US president twice. Cont

stories.title: Wife of US heir and activist donor on fighting his extradition to US: ‘It’s incredible that this can happen’
stories.summary: Stella Schnabel says Trump administration is falsely accusing James ‘Fergie’ Chambers of contributing to Hamas Although her husband, James “Fergie” Chambers, had been locked up in Spain for nearly a week, Stella Schnabel didn’t break down and cry until Thursday, when she finally got to speak to him for several minutes – enough time, she said, “for us to say we love each other and for him to say: ‘Tell the kids I love them.’” Spanish authorities, operating on a US extradition request, arrested th

stories.title: Cuba edges toward breakdown as power cuts and US meddling push society to brink
stories.summary: As Cuba swelters under six-month oil blockade imposed by US, tempers are fraying and unrest is growing When Cuba’s national grid collapses, as it did for the third time in 10 days on Tuesday, a collective groan spreads across its cities and people wonder, again, whether the island’s antiquated electricity system may soon become unrecoverable. The 777-mile Caribbean island of 9.5 million people has been sweltering under a six-month-long oil blockade imposed by the US, part of a pressure campaign

stories.title: Iran: US attacks show Trump's word is 'utterly worthless'
stories.summary: Iran has issued a written statement attributed to new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei. It rebukes the recent US strikes and warns of "unforgettable lessons." DW has the latest.

stories.title: Israel: How real is threat of gender segregation on street?
stories.summary: An Israeli city, with a large ultra-Orthodox Jewish population, wants to separate men and women on certain streets. Israeli politicians voted to allow university courses to do the same.

stories.title: Norway: Huge fire destroys 100 homes, sparks evacuations
stories.summary: Firefighters from other areas of Norway were drafted in to help tackle the blaze in the southern city of Drammen.

stories.title: Germany: CDU's Jens Spahn resigns amid surrogacy controversy
stories.summary: Surrogacy is not permitted in Germany and the conservative Christian Democratic Union (CDU) opposes its legalization. Spahn and his husband had their child through a surrogate mother in the US.

stories.title: Cuban dissident artist arrives in US after 5 years in prison
stories.summary: Luis Manuel Otero Alcantara, a prominent Cuban artist and dissident, was detained in July 2021 as he attempted to join unprecedented mass protests across the communist island.

stories.title: France's Mbappe sets World Cup scoring record with 22 goals
stories.summary: Kylian Mbappe has moved past Lionel Messi on the all-time World Cup goal-scoring record in a spectacular match for third place. England won the game 6-4.

stories.title: Andrew Tate, brother arrested in Miami, UK seeks extradition
stories.summary: Andrew and Tristan Tate were taken into custody by the US Marshals Service in Miami on a sealed warrant. The UK has requested their extradition on charges that include trafficking and rape.

stories.title: Amid elections, Sao Tome and Principe balances global powers
stories.summary: As voters head to the polls, Sao Tome and Principe is attracting international scrutiny. A military agreement with Russia has heightened interest in the strategically located archipelago.

stories.title: India news: CJP to call for Modi's resignation after Sonam Wangchuk 'forcefully' shifted to hospital
stories.summary: Abhijeet Dipke said the Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) will now demand the resignation of Prime Minister Narendra Modi after Sonam Wangchuk was forcibly shifted to a hospital by police. DW has more.

stories.title: German politician resigns over surrogacy child controversy
stories.summary: Jens Spahn faced criticism over his use of a surrogate, a ban on which he previously backed.

stories.title: Deaths from Venezuela earthquakes rise above 5,000
stories.summary: Venezuela has been able to unlock €302 million of funding from the International Monetary Fund to support recovery and reconstruction following the devastating earthquakes that hit the northern coastal region last month.

stories.title: Trump threatens new Canada tariffs over fires sending 'filthy' air into US cities
stories.summary: Canadian leader Mark Carney says both the US and Canada have an equal responsibility to fight climate change, which experts say are worsening wildfire conditions.

stories.title: Two US troops killed in Jordan by Iranian attacks, US military says
stories.summary: The two servicemen were killed Friday as the US and partner forces defended against Iranian ballistic missile and drone attacks, according to a statement from the US military.

stories.title: Private jets flock to Montana - but locals can't afford the trailer park
stories.summary: Rents in Bozeman, Montana have skyrocketed as wealthy out-of-towners flock to the city to experience the American West.

stories.title: Russians turn to cash, putting more strain on slowing wartime economy
stories.summary: Russians have been hit by mobile internet shutdowns and more businesses are seeking to dodge tax after more than four years of war with Ukraine.

stories.title: Tate brothers arrested in US after more charges laid against them in UK
stories.summary: The Crown Prosecution Service said it would seek to extradite the brothers from the US, while their lawyer dismissed the new charges as "slander".

stories.title: US forces strike Iran for seventh consecutive night
stories.summary: Iran's Fars state news agency reported that Iranian forces had responded by attacking US allies and bases in the region, including the Ali Al Salem airbase in Kuwait.

stories.title: ICE arrests human rights lawyer who fled Chinese crackdown
stories.summary: Arrest in Pennsylvania of Wu Shaoping, who is awaiting asylum decision, raises fears of deportation and persecution A Chinese human rights lawyer has been arrested by US Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE), raising concerns he could be deported to China where he would face persecution. Wu Shaoping fled China at the end of 2019 amid a crackdown on human rights lawyers. He travelled to the US on a tourist visa and made an asylum claim in 2020, for which he is still awaiting a decision. Conti

stories.title: Seven Americans quarantining at Kenya Ebola facility after US travel ban, says aid group
stories.summary: Aid workers are first known people to quarantine at facility, which sparked huge opposition in Kenya Seven American aid workers who had been in Congo to fight the Ebola outbreak are quarantining at a new isolation facility in ⁠Kenya after the US government introduced travel ⁠restrictions, the head of a US charity ​employing them told Reuters. The aid workers are the first known people to quarantine at the facility, which has sparked huge opposition in Kenya and is at the heart of a legal case in

stories.title: Europe’s most effective tool to cut greenhouse gas emissions ‘risks being weakened’
stories.summary: European Commission proposal to overhaul emissions trading system would give companies less demanding pathway to reductions Europe’s most effective method of cutting dangerous planet-heating gases risks being weakened after the European Commission proposed an overhaul of its flagship carbon market, critics have said. In a long-awaited review of the European Union emissions trading system (ETS), the European Commission proposed giving companies a less demanding and cheaper pathway to reduce green

stories.title: Israeli strike on Gaza funeral killed at least seven people, hospital says
stories.summary: Another 22 reportedly injured while mourning Palestinian killed in Israeli attack earlier in the day An Israeli strike on a funeral in the Gaza Strip has killed at least seven people and injured another 22, according to a local hospital. There was no immediate comment from the Israeli military. Continue reading...

stories.title: Many Ukrainian soldiers outraged over removal of defence minister, troops tell BBC
stories.summary: Protests erupted in Ukraine on Thursday after Mykhailo Fedorov's removal - and now soldiers are also criticising the move.

stories.title: The hill I will die on: Parisian waiters are not rude – they’re just badly misunderstood | Helen Massy-Beresford
stories.summary: After living here for years, I can see through that old stereotype. My tip: if your server is not full of bonhomie, why not try saying ‘bonjour’? Parisian waiters are professionals, providing an excellent service – they are not rude or unfriendly, just sometimes slightly misunderstood. No, really, hear me out. We’re all familiar with the trope of the rude Parisian waiter, looking down their nose at your inferior wine choice. They have been called “brusque and unwelcoming”, “snooty and rude” by t

stories.title: Andy Burnham considers radical shake-up to cut energy bills
stories.summary: Labour leader examining proposals to overhaul gas standing charges and make heat pumps cheaper to run than boilers Andy Burnham is considering radical plans that could cut household energy bills by £130 a year and make running a heat pump cheaper than a gas boiler. In his speech on Friday as he became the new Labour leader , Burnham promised to reduce the price of “essentials”, and a cost of living package is expected to be one of his first announcements in Downing Street. Continue reading...

stories.title: What will Keir Starmer do next?
stories.summary: Outgoing PM has joked about cookery classes and cutting hedges, but does the international stage beckon? As his time in Downing Street comes to an end, Keir Starmer has been joking with friends about what he might do after he stands down as prime minister. He has teased that he might take a cookery course. “He needs it, he only makes two meals,” one friend said. Another not entirely serious suggestion was cutting his father-in-law’s hedge in the expectation that if he did well, he could graduate

stories.title: UK tech advocates alarmed by Burnham plan to scrap technology department
stories.summary: MPs and industry experts say potential reorganisation will waste time at critical moment for AI and economic growth Andy Burnham’s plan to scrap the government’s technology department has triggered an angry backlash from MPs, Whitehall officials and tech experts. The incoming prime minister has asked officials to draw up plans to abolish the Department for Science, Innovation and Technology as part of a wider Whitehall shake-up. Continue reading...

stories.title: Billionaire US envoy faces Venice protests amid yacht tour
stories.summary: Hundreds protested the arrival of billionaire and Trump-appointed US Ambassador Tilman Fertitta in Venice. The ambassador's stop — on his personal yacht — is part of a tour marking 250 years of American independence.

stories.title: Germany news: Minister warns of high terror threat
stories.summary: Germany's interior minister has warned of a heightened security threat, saying an attack could be imminent. Meanwhile, a train security guard was critically injured after falling from a moving train. DW has the latest.

stories.title: Japan's superstitions make some homes hard to sell or rent
stories.summary: Many Japanese people are reluctant to buy or rent homes where a sudden or violent death has occurred. To reassure prospective buyers and tenants, property owners are calling in "ghost investigators."

stories.title: India launches first private rocket into space
stories.summary: Lifting off from an island north of Chennai, the Vikram-1 rocket signals India’s plans to take a larger share of the market for commercial rocket launches.

stories.title: Germany cracks down on money laundering, tax fraud
stories.summary: The German government plans to crack down on tax fraud, money laundering and illegally acquired assets with more audits and stiffer penalties.

stories.title: Why did Jens Spahn's surrogacy spark debate in Germany?
stories.summary: Jens Spahn, a key ally of Chancellor Friedrich Merz, is stepping down amid a controversy surrounding his decision to have a child via a surrogate mother. He had opposed legalizing surrogacy in Germany.

stories.title: Reproductive medicine laws in Germany 'shockingly unjust'
stories.summary: Infertility affects one in six women in Germany. However, access to reproductive medicine is not available to all. Surrogacy as well as egg donation are banned.

stories.title: Control, threats, disfiguring surgery: My life inside Jeffrey Epstein's 'cult'
stories.summary: "Anya" gives the BBC a rare account of how sex-criminal financier Epstein lured and abused his “assistants”.

stories.title: Indian activist on hunger strike for 20 days forcibly taken to hospital
stories.summary: Cockroach Janta Party founder Abhijeet Dipke has now begun an indefinite fast in Sonam Wangchuk's place.

stories.title: Space start-up launches India’s first commercial rocket designed for orbit
stories.summary: India's first space-tech unicorn Skyroot Aerospace successfully launched Vikram-1 on Saturday.

stories.title: Helicopters dump water as huge Norway fire destroys homes
stories.summary: The blaze has destroyed more than 100 homes in Drammen and has spread to a nearby forest, authorities say.

stories.title: Laos says it can't determine cause of tourist deaths linked to methanol
stories.summary: Officials say they have no evidence of poisoning over the 2024 deaths because autopsies were not conducted.

stories.title: „Számolni kell a merényletek kockázatával” – emelték a terrorfenyegetettség szintjét Németországban
stories.summary: Az eddigi „elméletit” emelték most magas szintre.

stories.title: Ha elég a tengerpartból, vár a horvát boszorkányok hegye
stories.summary: A tenger felé autózva a spliti autópályán, Ogulin városa környékén feltűnik egy érdekes formájú sziklacsúcs, ami élesen kiválik dimbes-dombos környezetéből. Ez az 1181 méteres Klek, ami szinte kiabál, hogy gyere, koma, másszál meg. Nem csoda, hogy ez a horvát hegymászás bölcsője, miként az sem, hogy a karakteres sziklacsúcsot számos sötét legenda övezi.

stories.title: Sokkal magasabb kamatot ad a Revolut az újonnan regisztrált ügyfeleknek
stories.summary: A július 14-én indult promócióban az új számlát nyitók 6 hónapon keresztül 4,25 százalékos éves kamatot kapnak.

stories.title: Az USA zsinórban hetedik napja támadja Iránt, ami válaszul Amerikával szövetséges országokat vett célba
stories.summary: Irán szerint nincs semmi, ami megállíthatná az USA „barbárságát”, Amerika pedig annyit közölt, hogy „felelősségre vonják” Iránt.

stories.title: Antonelli brutálisan jó körrel nyerte a belga időmérőt, négy pilótát is büntettek motorcsere miatt
stories.summary: Norris és Stroll 10-10, Alonso 20, Hadjar 30 rajthelyes büntetést kapott a motoralkatrészek cseréi miatt. Verstappen kezdi majd Antonelli mellől a futamot.

stories.title: Karácsony Gergely társadalmi párbeszédre bocsátja a Szabadság téren lévő német megszállási emlékmű jövőjét
stories.summary: A főpolgármester azt mondta: a szobor 12 éve mérgezi a közterületet és a közéletet.

stories.title: Davor Šuker: Tuchel baromságokat beszél
stories.summary: A horvát focilegendát felhúzta, hogy az angolok szövetségi kapitánya lekicsinylően nyilatkozott a világbajnokság bronzmeccséről.

stories.title: Az új popgeneráció legerősebb hangja mintha a hatvanas évekből érkezett volna
stories.summary: A húszéves Sienna Spiro hangja Nancy Sinatrát, öltözködése a hatvanas éveket idézi, sokan már most azt mondják, ő az új Adele. Az ördög Pradát visel 2 betétdala és első lemeze alapján hamar popikon státuszba emelkedhet.

stories.title: Trump újabb vámokkal fenyegeti Kanadát, mert az erdőtűzeik miatt „mocskos” az amerikai városok levegője
stories.summary: Ontario miniszterelnöke inkább segítséget kérne az USA-tól.

stories.title: Sulyok Tamás aláírta az Alaptörvény-módosítást, hétfőtől nem ő lesz a köztársasági elnök
stories.summary: A törvény kihirdetésével megszűnik Polt Péter és három másik alkotmánybíró megbízatása is, a vármegyékből ismét megyék lesznek. Az Országgyűlés harminc napon belül választ új köztársasági elnököt, az ellenzéknek ebbe se lesz beleszólása.

stories.title: A spanyol válogatott szövetségi kapitánya egyszer megpróbált emberfogással védekezni Messi ellen, négy gólt kaptak
stories.summary: Luis de la Fuente felidézte, milyen volt az első találkozása az argentin legendával.

stories.title: „Az intézmény iránti tiszteletem végső pecsétje” – videón Sulyok Tamás bejelentése
stories.summary: Aláírja az Alaptörvény 17. módosítását, ami megszüntette a megbízatását.

stories.title: Török Gábor Sulyok aláírásáról: Nem ért egyet, de aláírja, ahogy eddig is tette, mindig, mindenkor
stories.summary: A politikai elemző szerint Sulyok Tamás még egyszer és minden bizonnyal utoljára bizonyítja, hogy teljesen alkalmatlan volt a feladatára.

stories.title: Political Capital Sulyok Tamás távozásáról: Ezt a csatát a kormány megnyerte
stories.summary: Az elemzőintézet szerint ha az új államfő tekintélyes és autonóm karakter lesz, akkor Sulyok leváltása utólag is indokoltnak tűnik majd.

stories.title: Sulyok aláírta. De mi jön most?
stories.summary: Nincs megállás, még a nyáron kiválasztja az Országgyűlés az új államfőt.

stories.title: Hadházy egy fotó alapján azt állítja: már korábban is pusztultak el zebrák Hatvanpusztán
stories.summary: A képet beadta feljelentés-kiegészítésként a rendőrségnek.

stories.title: Pósfai Gábor: A legfontosabb feladat a bérrendezés, de gyakori visszajelzés a rendőröktől a nem megfelelő ruházat is
stories.summary: Reagált a belügyminiszter a rendőrök új egyenruháira érkezett megjegyzésekre.

stories.title: Szabó Bence átadta felajánlását a Hintalovon Alapítványnak, ami a neki összegyűjtött adományokból jött össze
stories.summary: A márciusi gyűjtésen több mint 279 millió forint jött össze a korábbi nyomozónak.

stories.title: Trump kritizálta az angol válogatott túl védekező taktikáját, nem érti, miért vonták annyira hátra Kane-t
stories.summary: Az amerikai elnök már futballtanácsokat is szívesen osztogat.

stories.title: Megveszett a világ
stories.summary: A kongói szoborszurkolótól a takarító japánokon keresztül a norvég vikingevezésig ismét tele volt emlékezetes szurkolói megmozdulásokkal a foci-vb. A legjobb szurkolói momentumokból válogattunk.

stories.title: Forsthoffer Ágnes: Minden erőmmel azon leszek, hogy a rám ruházott feladatokat kiegyensúlyozottan, pártatlanul és az ország érdekeit szolgálva végezzem
stories.summary: Sulyok Tamás után ideiglenesen az Országgyűlés elnöke lesz a köztársasági elnök.

stories.title: Franciaország–Anglia 4-6
Két amerikai katona meghalt, egy pedig eltűnt a Jordániát ért iráni légitámadásban
stories.summary: Irán és az Egyesült Államok ismét egymást lövi, miután múlt héten összeomlott a felek közötti tűzszüneti megállapodás.

stories.title: Anglia hatvan év után nyert világbajnoki érmet, tízgólos meccsen verték a franciákat
stories.summary: Tízgólos, őrült fordulatokat hozó meccsen Anglia szerezte meg a bronzérmet a 2026-os futball-világbajnokságon. Az első félidő után már 4-0-ra vezettek az angolok, de a szünet után a franciák felzárkóztak, és az egyenlítéshez is nagyon közel jártak. Végül az angol válogatott 6-4-re nyert és 60 év után szereztek ismét világbajnoki érmet. Kylian Mbappé két gólt lőtt a meccsen, amivel vb-rekordot döntött.

stories.title: Australia updates travel advice for Laos as charges announced over mass methanol poisoning
stories.summary: Distillery owner could face only three months in jail over alleged poisoning of Australian teenagers Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The Australian government has increased its travel alert level for Laos, advising travellers to “exercise a high degree of caution” due to the risk of methanol poisoning. It comes as a distillery owner in Laos was charged after a mass methanol poisoning incident killed six foreign nationals, including the Australian backpackers Bianca J

stories.title: Moroccan intelligence insider reveals widespread use of Pegasus hacking software
stories.summary: Whistleblower suggests internal security services deployed spyware from 2017 against key domestic and foreign targets A former member of Morocco’s domestic intelligence service has helped to provide an unprecedented insight into how the north African state used hacking software – including Pegasus spyware – to target journalists, human rights defenders, French politicians and Spanish cabinet ministers and police officers. Pegasus, which is manufactured by the Israel-based NSO Group, allows its o

stories.title: Inside the White House debate over Trump’s elections speech
Trump revives claims of election interference during address to nation
stories.summary: The US president said he would declassify intelligence data that revealed China had illicitly acquired 220 million US voter files. Trump's claim contradicts reviews that have found no evidence of significant interference in the 2020 election.

stories.title: Watch: US Navy Blue Angels' low flyover leaves beachgoers stunned
stories.summary: US Navy Blue Angels jets flew low over a crowded Florida beach, startling beachgoers as chairs and tents were blown away and children covered their ears. The dramatic flyover sparked renewed debate over the safety of low-altitude military demonstrations.

stories.title: Euronews aboard EU warships in the Red Sea: Europe eyes wider mandate as Houthi threat grows
stories.summary: In a special report from EU patrol ships in Djibouti, Kaja Kallas told Euronews that the 27-member bloc was discussing expanding Aspides' mandate and that ships pledged to a Hormuz coalition could be redeployed to the Red Sea in the interim.

stories.title: Ukraine lawmakers approve Koretskyi as new PM amid protests
stories.summary: It is President Volodymyr Zelenskyy's second Cabinet shakeup in a year. He faces criticism for dismissing reformist Defense Minister Mykhailo Fedorov.

stories.title: US slaps 25% tariffs on Brazil with election looming
stories.summary: The US will impose a 25% tariff on most imports from Brazil starting later this month. It's the first action in the Trump administration's new tariff strategy, after the Supreme Court struck down its prior impositions.

stories.title: Germany massively expands federal police powers
stories.summary: The German government has increased the powers of its federal police, especially in the use of AI, surveillance and drone defenses. But experts warn of constitutional challenges.

stories.title: Iran launches retaliatory attacks on US bases in Kuwait, Bahrain and Jordan
stories.summary: The latest missile strikes began when the US said it fired a missile into a Curaçao-flagged oil tanker's funnel after claiming the ship "ignored multiple warnings".

stories.title: Kyiv hit again as Ukraine reports two dead in Russian strike
stories.summary: Russian missiles killed two people and wounded five, including a child, in Kyiv, Ukraine, on 16 July 2026.

stories.title: Kyiv protesters rally after Zelenskyy sacks defence minister Fedorov
stories.summary: Hundreds protest in Kyiv after President Zelenskyy dismisses Defence Minister Fedorov, a moderniser credited with slowing Russia’s advance.

stories.title: China ‘strongly dissatisfied’ with nationalisation of British Steel
stories.summary: Move dealt ‘severe blow to Chinese companies’ confidence in investing in the UK’, says Ministry of Commerce China’s government has said it is “strongly dissatisfied” with the decision to nationalise British Steel this week, 15 months after the UK government intervened to prevent the closure of its steelworks in Scunthorpe and the loss of 4,000 jobs. On Thursday, British Steel was brought under public ownership to protect “the future of steel production”, the government announced. Continue readin

stories.title: Biodiversity fears as human-bred hybrid fish integrate into Philippines lake
stories.summary: Escaped flowerhorn cichlids are causing concern for native species and about parasites capable of infecting humans Escaped ornamental aquarium fish have integrated into a local ecosystem in the Philippines, but scientists say they may be threatening the native biodiversity of the lake. Flowerhorn cichlids – human-bred hybrid fish prized for their bright-gold colour and prominent head humps – are believed to have escaped from breeding facilities into Lake Sampaloc, which sits in a volcanic crater

stories.title: Cuban dissident artist Luis Manuel Otero Alcántara cleared to travel to US
stories.summary: Whereabouts of artist imprisoned after 2021 protests had been unknown following end of his prison term in July Luis Manuel Otero Alcántara, a prominent Cuban dissident artist whose whereabouts remained unknown after his prison sentence ended last week, has been given permission to travel to the US, according to his official Facebook page. Otero Alcántara, designated a prisoner of conscience by Amnesty International, was sentenced to five years in prison in 2022 for insulting national symbols, co

stories.title: Ughy Szabina: Férjem, a színész
stories.summary: „Én nem tudtam, hogy a férjem ekkora színész. De magamról sem tudtam, hogy ilyen ügyes színésznő vagyok. Darabunk címe: A hűség.” E heti novellánk egy házasságból villant fel jeleneteket.

stories.title: Majd csak a vb után büntetheti meg a FIFA a bírókat kritizáló játékosokat és edzőket
stories.summary: Didier Deschamps francia szövetségi kapitány a Spanyolország elleni elődöntő után megkérdőjelezte, hogy a játékvezető alkalmas volt-e egy ilyen rangadó levezetésére, de több ilyen eset is volt.

stories.title: Bekapcsolták a minisztériumban a technikát az élőzéshez, a Lázárinfó volt rajta beállítva
stories.summary: Vitézy Dávid szerint egyértelmű, hogy Lázár kampányrendezvényeit a minisztérium fizette.

stories.title: Dróngyárat és raktárakat támadtak Oroszország területén az ukránok
stories.summary: Zelenszkij szerint a frontvonaltól több mint 700 kilométerre is sikerrel jártak.

stories.title: Új, modern egyenruhát kapnak a rendőrök
stories.summary: Nem lesz könnyű dolguk a pályázóknak, például egy sima tányérsapkának is 12 követelménynek kell megfelelnie.

stories.title: Kovács Gergely nem indul, eddig egyetlen jelentkező van a Kétfarkú Kutya Párt elnöki posztjára
stories.summary: Tóth-Benedek Csanád szerint a pártnak vissza kell térni a természetéhez, a tartozások rendezését nevezte a legfontosabb feladatnak.

stories.title: Karácsony Gergelynek is van egy ötlete a 16 éves választói korhatárra
stories.summary: Azt javasolja, hogy egyelőre az önkormányzati választásra legyen érvényes.

stories.title: Ha véletlen eldurran egy trafóállomás vagy kisiklik egy vonat, annak valószínűleg ezekhez a gyárakhoz lesz köze
stories.summary: Az EU drónmegállapodást kötött az ukránokkal, aminek már idén hatása lehet az ukrán fronton, de az európai gyárak és raktárak az orosz hibrid hadviselés célpontjába kerülhetnek. Az rövid távon inkább csak gesztus Trumptól, hogy az ukránok Patriot légvédelmi rakétákat is gyárthatnak majd, de évek múlva ez komoly stratégiai előny lesz Ukrajnának.

stories.title: Iran's oil supply threat extends beyond Strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: Oil and gas producers in the Gulf are seeking alternatives to the Strait of Hormuz as traffic again comes under fire. But is Iran also turning its attention to the pipelines meant to bypass the waterway?

stories.title: How AI and satellites help fight wildfires
stories.summary: Wildfires are raging across Europe, Canada and beyond. To successfully fight these fires, speed is essential. A German startup uses satellite data and AI to spot fires early.

stories.title: The EU climate policy the world copied is under fire — what's at stake?
stories.summary: The EU Commission has proposed to weaken Europe's emissions trading system, a global blueprint for pricing carbon and cutting CO2. How effective is the scheme and where does it fall short?

stories.title: How new PM Andy Burnham will change UK's international role
stories.summary: From northwest England to the global stage: Andy Burnham becomes Britain's new prime minister on Monday. How will the former mayor of Manchester tackle international affairs?

stories.title: Meet Andy Burnham, Britain's next prime minister
stories.summary: Andy Burnham was confirmed as Keir Starmer's successor as British prime minister. The former Manchester mayor became a leading figure in the Labour Party and one of the UK's most popular politicians.

stories.title: Cities are getting smarter about water and other eco wins
stories.summary: From flood-resistant infrastructure to new ways of securing drinking water, cities are adapting to the growing climate pressures of a hotter, drier world. Plus, tourists pitching in to help sea turtles.

stories.title: Merz and Macron: A Franco-German week of harmony
stories.summary: Friedrich Merz and Emmanuel Macron did everything they could this week to demonstrate a close partnership. This is likely because both fear that the next French president could be nationalist Marine Le Pen.

stories.title: Germany's 5,000 heat deaths: What the numbers reveal
stories.summary: More than 5,100 people in Germany died from heat-related causes this year until the end of June, the Robert Koch Institute reports. But how can researchers prove that someone died because of soaring temperatures?

stories.title: US airstrikes hit Iranian civilian sites as Tehran attacks Gulf neighbours
stories.summary: In a primetime address to the nation, Trump said the US was "winning big in Iran" and the public would see "the fruits of that labour". However, a deal seems far out of reach as fighting in the region continues to escalate.

stories.title: Japan revises imperial succession law but retains ban on female emperors
stories.summary: Nationwide opinion polls in Japan have suggested broad public support for a female emperor, but some conservative political figures remain opposed to the idea.

stories.title: Trump Media to sell instant access to 'market-moving' social posts
stories.summary: Trump Media is launching a fast, paid feed to its most influential posts for Wall Street traders.

stories.title: Qatar’s air defence intercepts fresh Iranian missile barrage over Doha
stories.summary: Iran has unleashed a new wave of attacks across the Gulf, targeting Qatar, Kuwait, Bahrain and Jordan, following Tehran’s continuous threats of dragging the Gulf countries back into its escalating confrontation with the US over the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: Chile assesses damage after deadly storm surge floods coastal city of Penco
stories.summary: Chile's central coast has been hit by an exceptional storm surge that flooded homes and damaged infrastructure. Authorities are urging residents to stay away from the shoreline as dangerous sea conditions continue.

stories.title: Kim Jong Un hosts top Chinese official in Pyongyang
stories.summary: China has sought to reassert its position in the Korean peninsula in recent years. Chinese President Xi Jinping made his own visit to Pyongyang in June as part of this push.

stories.title: Bodycam footage shows airlift of girl stranded by Texas floods
stories.summary: Footage shows a rescuer reaching a family of three stranded by floods that destroyed their house in Uvalde county.

stories.title: Trump alleges China meddled in 2020 election and questions voting security ahead of midterms
stories.summary: China said Trump's claims were fabricated and US Democrats said he was paving the way to undermine November's elections.

stories.title: CIA declassifies evidence of alleged Venezuelan election fraud in 2012
stories.summary: CIA documents declassified by Donald Trump confirm that the Venezuelan regime had, from 2012, a system capable of altering up to 1.5 million votes, though they do not prove fraud in every election mentioned.

stories.title: China rescuers race to find trapped people after landslide
stories.summary: China launched a large-scale rescue operation after a landslide struck Pengshui County in Chongqing municipality on 17 July, burying part of a residential area and trapping an unknown number of people.

stories.title: Xi hails 'new level' in ties as China and Kazakhstan sign €11.4bn in deals
stories.summary: Xi Jinping and Kassym-Jomart Tokayev signed more than 70 commercial agreements on the sidelines of the World AI Conference in Shanghai, including Central Asia's first battery plant with CATL and a new Caspian port terminal.

stories.title: China rejects Trump's election interference accusations as 'groundless'
stories.summary: At a daily briefing in Beijing, a spokesperson called on the US to stop making what it described as 'groundless accusations' against China.

stories.title: World Cup organisers 'monitoring' wildfire smoke ahead of New Jersey final
stories.summary: Officials in many cities urged residents to stay indoors or wear masks outside as air quality reached unhealthy to hazardous levels, meaning it posed a health risk to everyone,

stories.title: 7.3 magnitude earthquake strikes Mexico and Guatemala coasts, tsunami alert lifted
stories.summary: A 7.3 magnitude earthquake shook the coasts of Mexico and Guatemala on Friday, its epicentre off Chiapas at about 15 km deep.

stories.title: EU foreign policy chief Kallas visits EU naval mission in the Red Sea
stories.summary: EU foreign policy chief Kaja Kallas visited personnel serving with the EU's ASPIDES and ATALANTA naval missions, receiving operational briefings and meeting troops deployed to help protect shipping routes in the Red Sea. Euronews correspondent Toby Gregory accompanied Kallas during the visit.

stories.title: Magnitude 7.3 earthquake quake strikes off Mexico coast
stories.summary: The US Tsunami Warning Center has recorded waves 0.3m (1.1 feet) high in Puerto Madero and Chiapas in Mexico after the quake.

stories.title: EU proposes slowing down cuts to carbon emissions for businesses
stories.summary: Under the proposals, the EU plans to relax its emissions trading system to give companies more time to reduce their carbon output.

stories.title: One anti-war critic fined, another held as Russia clamps down on dissent
stories.summary: While Boris Nadezhdin is barred from running for parliament, blogger Ilya Remeslo is remanded in custody.

stories.title: White House defends Argentina team over Falklands banner
stories.summary: The comments could further fuel the row over the incident, which has seen Downing Street back calls for Fifa to investigate.

stories.title: Kim Jong Un was meant to be their only idol - then North Koreans discovered K-pop
stories.summary: Defectors tell the BBC that despite restrictions, K-pop has cut through in the stifling dictatorship.

stories.title: How these twins' rare growth disorder could provide the key to preventing cancer
stories.summary: Researchers have found that people with Laron syndrome have a lower incidence of cancer than the wider population.

stories.title: What this desert city can teach the world about tackling heat deaths
stories.summary: Maricopa County in Arizona has some of the hottest temperatures, but it has reduced the number of heat-related deaths.

stories.title: He made history in Congress - then the Supreme Court changed its mind
stories.summary: Residents say they're afraid that if Shomari Figures loses his seat, their federal-funding could disappear with him.

stories.title: Brad Pitt's children are dropping their dad's surname - here's why I did the same
stories.summary: Hannah had used her mother's maiden name on her social media for years before she legally made the change.

stories.title: More than 500 feared dead after reports of two shipwrecks off Myanmar, UN says
stories.summary: Vessels believed to have departed from Myanmar in late June, with mostly Muslim Rohingya minority onboard The United Nations has said more than 500 people are feared dead after reports of two large shipwrecks off Myanmar since late June. The UN’s International Organization for Migration (IOM) and its refugee agency UNHCR voiced alarm in a joint statement at reports “that two boats carrying more than 500 people may have capsized off the coast of Myanmar in recent days”. Continue reading...

stories.title: East-West border death zone now Germany's biodiversity oasis
stories.summary: Many endangered species now live in the area that once formed the border between East and West Germany. Environment Minister Carsten Schneider recently visited the "Green Belt," and DW went along for the ride.

stories.title: AI: Why Europe is falling behind, and how it can catch up
stories.summary: Europe trails the US and China in artificial intelligence. Economist and Nobel laureate Philippe Aghion says better research funding, venture capital and AI innovation can help it catch up.

stories.title: Monaco assassination plot takes a new turn
stories.summary: A woman suspected of plotting to assassinate a Ukrainian-Cypriot businessman in Monaco has been found dead in Ukraine. Two men with ties to Ukraine's intelligence services have now been brought into custody.

stories.title: Tehran vows to 'crush' regional infrastructure if US hits Iranian sites
stories.summary: Iran said its response to any US strike on its infrastructure would result in destruction across the region, as multiple reports said Tehran had asked the Houthis to be ready to close the Bab el-Mandeb Strait, threatening a second global shipping chokepoint.

stories.title: Supporters welcome France's assisted dying law
stories.summary: Supporters welcomed France's assisted dying law after parliament approved the landmark legislation, calling it "a huge relief" for people with incurable illnesses. The law still requires approval from France's Constitutional Council.

stories.title: Watch: What to know as Genoa bridge disaster trial ends
stories.summary: More than 50 defendants have been on trial in Italy over the 2018 bridge collapse which killed 43 people.

stories.title: Trump teleprompter operator suspended for alleged prediction market bets on speeches
stories.summary: US media had reported that Gabriel Perez, who has been operating Trump’s teleprompter since 2016, used his inside knowledge to win more than $100,000 (€85,900) betting on what the president would say in big speeches.

stories.title: Uganda calls for travel restrictions to be lifted after last Ebola patient discharged
stories.summary: Country begins 42-day countdown to outbreak being declared officially over, as numbers continue to rise in neighbouring Democratic Republic of the Congo Uganda has started lobbying countries to lift Ebola-related travel restrictions after discharging its last confirmed Ebola patient from hospital. The discharge of a Congolese national from the Mulago national referral hospital’s isolation centre in Kampala on Thursday triggered the start of a 42-day countdown required by the World Health Organiz

stories.title: UK aid cuts ‘reduce bilateral support to some African countries by 90%’
stories.summary: Critics say Foreign Office figures send ‘global message about the role the country wants to play on international stage’ Labour’s foreign aid cuts mean reductions of as much as 90% in the bilateral support the UK will give to some African countries, Foreign Office figures show. The department’s annual report includes a long-awaited breakdown of how the reduction in the aid budget will affect individual countries for the next three years. Continue reading...

stories.title: Could Russia be about to lose its grip on chess leadership?
stories.summary: Russians have long pulled the strings when it comes to the governing body of international chess. But now two Germans are vying for FIDE's top job.

stories.title: Will gold prices extend their record-breaking run?
stories.summary: Predictions suggest the price of the precious metal will keep hitting new record highs as investors seek a safe bet in turbulent times. Some forecasts say prices could double within five years.

stories.title: Germany may make it illegal to deny Israel's right to exist
stories.summary: Germany's upper house of parliament, the Bundesrat, has introduced a bill seeking to ban the questioning of Israel's right to exist. It is now up to the lower house to decide whether to turn it into law.

stories.title: German tenants need better heat protection
stories.summary: Heat waves are becoming longer, hotter and more frequent. Record temperatures have left many tenants struggling. Now Chancellor Friedrich Merz has said the building law needs to be amended.

stories.title: Buenos Aires erupts as Argentina reach World Cup final
stories.summary: Fans flooded the streets of Buenos Aires in celebration after Argentina came from behind to beat England 2-1.

stories.title: Argentina defeat England 2-1 in World Cup semi-final with a dramatic late double
stories.summary: The defending champions' victory means they will face Spain, who defeated France on Tuesday, in Sunday's final in East Rutherford, New Jersey.

stories.title: Argentina reaches World Cup final, jubilation in Buenos Aires and disappointment in the Falklands
stories.summary: Tens of thousands celebrate in Buenos Aires after Argentina’s 2-1 win over England sends them to the World Cup final against Spain.

stories.title: Toronto choked by wildfire smoke, records world's worst air quality
stories.summary: A thick orange haze hung over Toronto on Wednesday 15 July as smoke from wildfires in northwestern Ontario engulfed Canada's largest city, giving it the worst air quality of any major city in the world that morning, according to monitoring service IQAir.

stories.title: "It's freedom": Border residents react to end of Gibraltar-Spain checks
stories.summary: Routine border checks between Spain and Gibraltar have ended, allowing thousands of daily commuters to cross more easily. While many welcome shorter journeys and greater freedom, others remain cautious about the long-term impact.

stories.title: Canada: train crew escapes flames near Armstrong, Ontario
stories.summary: A train crew was surrounded by wildfire flames near Armstrong, Ontario, Canada, on 13 July 2026. The Canadian National Railway crew escaped safely as more than 160 fires burned across the region.

stories.title: China is 'a critical long-term strategic challenge', EU ministers say
stories.summary: EU foreign ministers issued a joint assessment of the risks posed by China, warning that Beijing threatens not only the bloc’s economy but also its security, while highlighting the shared ambition of China and Russia to assert regional dominance.
